decisions.title: Prioritize GPT-5.1 evaluation for Ask SIFT before production rollout
decisions.context: ENG-10352 indicates we're running evals against GPT-5.1. Need to decide threshold for production deployment given our North Star of Decisions Captured Per Customer—better model quality = more trust = more usage.

arguments.for: Better reasoning quality could significantly increase Ask SIFT adoption and decision capture rate. Early mover advantage with GPT-5.1.
arguments.against: Cost implications unclear. May need more eval time before production. Could wait for pricing stability.

decisions.title: Scope Sift Edge enum/units/descriptions feature set for V1 vs V2
decisions.context: Four related tickets (ENG-10355, ENG-10356, ENG-8857) suggest comprehensive metadata support for Sift Edge. Need to decide scope to balance 'Embed Deeper' pillar with shipping velocity.

arguments.for: Ship all metadata features together for coherent customer experience. Avoids migration pain later.
arguments.against: Phased rollout reduces risk, gets core value (enums) to customers faster, validates use cases before building full feature set.

decisions.title: FedRAMP certification timeline and resource commitment
decisions.context: ENG-9662 (FedRAMP requirements for Amplitude) and GTM-117 (Federal lander) signal enterprise moat-building opportunity. Major strategic decision on certification timing.
decisions.blockedBy: Need cost estimate and customer pipeline assessment from Sales for federal opportunities

arguments.for: Build the Moat pillar—FedRAMP is massive competitive barrier. Federal customers represent high-value, sticky revenue.
arguments.against: 18-24 month certification process. Significant eng/ops burden. Uncertain pipeline size. May distract from core product velocity.

decisions.title: Consolidate calculated channel naming/versioning strategy
decisions.context: ENG-9807 (cache key missing version causing stale data) and ENG-8512 (treat same-name calc channels as single) indicate fundamental design tension needing resolution.

arguments.for: Treating same-name channels as single entity simplifies UX, matches user mental model. Critical for 'Nail the Core' reliability.
arguments.against: Versioning enables experimentation and rollback. Collapsing loses history. Cache invalidation becomes more complex.

decisions.title: Canvas markdown support scope and editor selection
decisions.context: ENG-10324 signals Canvas enhancement. Decision needed on markdown flavor, editor library, and collaboration features to support Signal>Evidence>Decision workflow.

arguments.for: Full CommonMark + tables/mermaid enables rich decision documentation. Industry standard. Easy to export/import.
arguments.against: Rich editor adds complexity. May over-engineer for initial use cases. Could start with basic formatting.

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy context-switching week: 31 engineering signals across 6 product areas demand architectural decisions. Edge metadata features clustering. FedRAMP work beginning to surface cross-functionally.
alerts: Only 8 free hours this week—20% capacity. Strategy decisions require deep work time.
Three calc channel bugs/features suggest systemic design issue needing dedicated architecture time
FedRAMP scope creep risk: appearing in both eng backlog and GTM without formal decision
Ask SIFT model evaluation happening without clear success criteria or rollout plan
